title: Game story (stateful choice game)
description: Machine-read source of truth for a choice-based GAME (a branching story with state) at projects/{slug}/game/{lang}/{slug}.game.json. Unlike interactive-fiction (a pure node graph), a game tracks state: declared boolean flags + an item inventory, choices that are AVAILABLE only when state satisfies their requirements, and effects that mutate state. A scene's outcome (win/lose/neutral) marks a terminal state; a game needs at least one reachable win. The deterministic gate (scripts/check-game-story.mjs) enforces structural + state integrity: unique ids, real start, every choice target + every referenced flag/item resolves, no statically unsatisfiable requirement, and — over a BFS of (scene x state) — a reachable win, every scene reachable, and no reachable non-ending scene that is a softlock (a stuck state with no available choice). State is restricted to flags + items (no open-ended numeric stats) so winnability is decidable.

goal.description: Optional one-line player goal, shown in the rendered .md's subtitle (e.g. 'get out of the room alive').

start.description: Scene id the player begins at; must exist in scenes.

flags.description: Declared boolean world-state variables. A flag must be declared here before any choice may require or set it.

items.description: Declared inventory items. An item must be declared here before any choice may require, add, or remove it. Items start un-held (the player has none at the start).

scenes.description: Every scene in the game. scene.id is unique; choices reference other scene ids. A scene with an outcome (or ending:true, or no choices) is terminal.

id.description: Stable slug id; referenced by start + choice.target.

title.description: Human-readable scene heading for the rendered .md (e.g. 'The Study'). Optional; the renderer falls back to the id.

text.description: The scene prose the player sees.

ending.description: True => a terminal scene. A scene with an outcome, or with no choices, is terminal too.

outcome.description: Marks a terminal scene's result. A terminal scene must not also have choices. The game needs >= 1 reachable win.

choices.description: Branches out of this scene. Omit (or empty) for a terminal scene. A choice is AVAILABLE only when its requires are met in the current state; taking it applies its effects then moves to target.

requires_flags.description: Flag conditions that must hold for this choice to be available.

requires_items.description: Declared item ids the player must hold for this choice to be available.

set_flags.description: Effect: flags to set when this choice is taken.

add_items.description: Effect: declared item ids to add to inventory.

remove_items.description: Effect: declared item ids to remove from inventory.
